rationale.R1.1: The artifact closes with an explicit contribution claim (strategy-selection taxonomy, reframing the RL-vs-optimization debate); evaluating whether that claim is well-supported and accurately scoped by the body of the paragraph is central to any substantive review.
rationale.R1.2: The argument chains threshold values (0.95, 0.90, 0.70, 0.55) to regime conclusions and then to a deployment-risk assertion; logical consistency between these steps—e.g., why CMDP reaching zero at 0.70 is presented after a 0.55 figure—warrants scrutiny.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative figures (63%, 20%, threshold breakpoints) are stated as facts; verifying that each figure coheres with the implied experimental record and is not selectively framed is a natural expectation when reviewing a results paragraph.
rationale.R1.6: 'Take a look at this paragraph' in a writing context most naturally invites prose-level feedback; sentence clarity, internal consistency of terminology (e.g., 'feasibility' used both as a binary gate and a continuous percent), and paragraph flow are all surface-level concerns a reader would raise.
